title: Resource Downloads & Digital Credential
objectives: Download the U.S. Market Entry Checklist, Florida Setup Roadmap, Central Florida Resource Guide, and Expansion Decision Worksheet
Understand the POK Tech digital credential and how to display it on LinkedIn

contentBlocks.title: Your Takeaway Resources from Campus HQ
contentBlocks.content:
With your readiness assessment complete, think of this as walking through the [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu) resource center before heading out. You would not leave campus without picking up the materials that turn everything you learned into a real plan. These four downloadable resources do exactly that:

  1. U.S. Market Entry Checklist . Module-by-module action list from legal setup to ecosystem resources.
  2. Florida Setup Roadmap . Step-by-step sequence from entity formation to fully operational.
  3. Central Florida Resource Guide . Directory of organizations, contacts, and programs for international founders.
  4. Expansion Decision Worksheet . Structured go/no-go framework to consider whether now is the right time.

Download all four now. They pair directly with your 1:1 meeting with [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu).

contentBlocks.title: Did You Know?
contentBlocks.content: The UCF Business Incubation Program operates multiple sites across the greater Orlando region, providing over 100,000 square feet of incubation space. Since its founding, companies in the program have created thousands of jobs in Central Florida. UCF itself is one of the top patent-producing universities in the country, and the research partnerships between campus labs and incubator companies give founders access to talent and technology that would cost a fortune to build independently.

contentBlocks.title: Resource 1: U.S. Market Entry Checklist
contentBlocks.content:
This is your pre-departure checklist — every item, verified. The U.S. Market Entry Checklist turns every major topic from this program into action items grouped by module: legal setup, finances, IP protection, team building, market entry, culture, and ecosystem resources. Each item includes a priority level and suggested timeframe.

What does that mean for you? Tip: Start with the ecosystem section — it connects you with free resources before you start paying for services.

contentBlocks.title: Resource 2: Florida Setup Roadmap
contentBlocks.content:
This is your step-by-step sequence — what comes first, second, third. The Florida Setup Roadmap is sorted by order instead of topic, answering exactly that question. It walks through four phases: (1) pre-arrival preparation (remote), (2) entity formation and registration, (3) financial infrastructure, and (4) operational readiness. Each phase includes estimated timeframes and flags which steps require physical presence versus remote completion.

Many founders complete phases 1 and 2 remotely before arriving, so they can open a bank account in one visit with all documents ready.

contentBlocks.title: Resource 3: Central Florida Resource Guide
contentBlocks.content:
This is your campus directory — every support organization, one call away. The Central Florida Resource Guide puts all the groups from Module 7 into a single, easy-to-search reference. It covers business advising (SBDC, SCORE, [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu)), incubators and accelerators, government programs (SBA, Enterprise Florida), professional services (attorneys, CPAs, banking contacts), networking and community organizations, and details on what comes after this course through [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu).

Each entry includes the organization name, what they offer, and their website. Bookmark it and update it as you discover new resources through networking.

contentBlocks.title: Resource 4: Expansion Decision Worksheet
contentBlocks.content:
This is your go/no-go decision — the honest assessment before you commit. The Expansion Decision Worksheet helps you decide whether to enter the U.S. market now. It scores you across five areas: market fit, financial readiness, legal readiness, operations, and timing.

This is not pass/fail. A founder from Colombia scored 2 out of 5 on financial readiness, spent four months strengthening home-market revenue, re-scored at 4, and entered the U.S. with a much stronger foundation. The worksheet tells you what gap to close first.

Complete this before your 1:1 meeting with [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu) — it helps the team give you targeted advice based on your specific readiness profile.

sections.heading: Legal & Formation
sections.content:
- **[SunBiz](https://sunbiz.org)** (sunbiz.org) — Florida business registration
- **[IRS EIN](https://www.irs.gov/businesses/small-businesses-self-employed/employer-id-numbers)** (irs.gov) — Free employer identification number
- **[LegalZoom](https://www.legalzoom.com)/[Incfile](https://www.incfile.com)** — Paid formation services if you want assistance
- **[Florida Bar Lawyer Referral](https://www.floridabar.org/public/lrs/)** — Find a vetted business attorney

sections.heading: Banking & Finance
sections.content:
- **[Mercury](https://mercury.com)** — Online banking for startups (international-friendly)
- **[Relay](https://relayfi.com)** — Free business banking with accounting integration
- **[Stripe Atlas](https://stripe.com/atlas)** — Formation + banking bundle for international founders
- **[QuickBooks Online](https://quickbooks.intuit.com)** — Most popular small business accounting software

sections.heading: Visas & Immigration
sections.content:
- **[USCIS](https://www.uscis.gov)** (uscis.gov) — Official immigration information
- **[Boundless](https://www.boundless.com)** — Immigration application assistance
- **[AILA Lawyer Search](https://www.ailalawyer.com)** — Find a qualified immigration attorney
- **[Fragomen](https://www.fragomen.com)** — International immigration law firm

sections.heading: Local Support
sections.content:
- **Florida [SBDC at UCF](https://sbdcorlando.com/)** — Free business consulting
- **[SCORE Orlando](https://orlando.score.org)** — Free mentorship from business veterans
- **[Orlando Economic Partnership](https://orlando.org)** — Relocation and expansion support
- **[Prospera](https://prosperausa.org/)** — Bilingual support for Hispanic entrepreneurs
- **[UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu)** — Business incubation programs

contentBlocks.title: Your Digital Credential
contentBlocks.content:
Your completion credential — proof you finished the program. When you complete this program, you earn a digital credential : a verified badge for your LinkedIn profile, website, or email signature. It validates completion of the [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu) U.S. Market Readiness Program, backed by one of the largest and most active university incubation ecosystems in the country.

Now, here is where it gets tricky. The credential is issued through POK Tech, the same provider used by Arizona State University and other major universities. It is a program completion credential, not a professional certification — but it signals you took a structured, university-backed program seriously.

How to Claim Your Credential

After completing the program, you receive an email from POK Tech. The process takes five minutes:

  1. Open the email and select the claim link
  2. Set up your POK Tech account (or log in)
  3. Accept the credential
  4. Share to LinkedIn using the built-in sharing tool

The credential does not expire.

contentBlocks.title: Entrepreneur organizing resources and preparation materials for U.S. market entry

questions.stem: A founder has validated her market opportunity and wants to know the correct ORDER of steps to become operational in Florida — from entity formation through financial setup to hiring. Which downloadable resource is specifically designed for this purpose?
options: U.S. Market Entry Checklist — it covers every topic from the program in checklist format
Florida Setup Roadmap — it organizes steps by sequence (what to do first, second, third) rather than by topic
Expansion Decision Worksheet — it helps assess whether to enter the U.S. market at all
Central Florida Resource Guide — it lists organizations and contacts available to founders
questions.explanation: The Florida Setup Roadmap is organized by sequence rather than by topic. It walks founders through four phases : pre-arrival preparation, entity formation, financial infrastructure, and operational readiness — in the correct order. The Checklist organizes by module topic, while the Roadmap answers 'what do I do first, second, third?'

questions.stem: A founder completed the Expansion Decision Worksheet and scored high on market fit but low on financial readiness. Based on the lesson guidance, what is the most strategic response?
options: Enter the U.S. market immediately since market fit is the most important factor
Abandon U.S. expansion plans entirely since financial readiness is insufficient
Strengthen home-market revenue first to improve financial readiness, then revisit the worksheet before entering the U.S.
Apply for an SBA loan to cover the financial gap before entering the market
questions.explanation: The Expansion Decision Worksheet is not a pass/fail test. A founder from Colombia used it, found her financial score was 2 out of 5, spent four months strengthening home-market revenue, and re-scored at 4 before entering the U.S. with a stronger foundation. The worksheet tells you what gap to close first, not whether to give up.

questions.stem: A founder from Brazil has completed the [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu) Phase 1 program and wants to use her digital credential to strengthen her professional profile when meeting potential U.S. partners. How should she apply the POK Tech credential?
options: Claim the credential through the POK Tech email link, add it to her LinkedIn profile using the built-in sharing tool, and reference it on her website — since the credential does not expire, it serves as an ongoing signal that she completed a structured, university-backed market readiness program
Print the credential and bring a physical copy to every business meeting as proof of her qualifications
Wait until she completes Phase 2 before claiming the credential, since the Phase 1 credential alone carries little weight
Use the credential to apply for SBA loans, since it serves as a professional certification recognized by federal agencies
questions.explanation: The POK Tech digital credential is designed to be shared digitally — on LinkedIn, websites, and email signatures. It validates completion of the [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu) U.S. Market Readiness Program and does not expire. It is a program completion credential, not a professional certification, so it does not qualify for SBA lending or serve as a formal qualification. There is no reason to wait for Phase 2 — the Phase 1 credential stands on its own as a signal of structured preparation.

contentBlocks.title: Key Takeaway
contentBlocks.content: The takeaway is this: do not leave headquarters without your equipment. These four resources are yours to keep, and they extend the value of this program beyond the screen and into your actual business planning. The checklist keeps you organized. The roadmap keeps you in sequence. The resource guide connects you to help. The decision worksheet keeps you honest about readiness. Apply these tools to your planning: download all four now, and bring them to your 1:1 meeting with the [UCF BIP](https://incubator.ucf.edu) team at campus headquarters. And claim your digital credential on LinkedIn. It is a small step, but it signals to everyone in your network that you are serious about doing this the right way.
